text: You choose the chair nearest the mantle. Norton doesn't look at you; he simply shifts his weight, his presence heavy and suffocating like the air in a deep shaft.

name: MainChar
text: It’s a long walk from the gates. You’d think a house this size could afford a bit more light in the drawing room.

name: Norton
text: Light is a luxury. If you’re looking for a warm welcome, you’ve walked into the wrong manor.

name: MainChar
text: I’ve had worse. At least the roof doesn't leak. Have you been here long, Mr. Campbell?

name: Norton
text: Long enough to know the furniture is sturdier than the promises made here. You’re better off worrying about your own business than my schedule.

name: MainChar
text: Just trying to be polite. It helps pass the time when the host is missing.

name: Norton
text: Politeness doesn't pay the bills, sweetheart. And time is the only thing we're actually spending here. Sit still and keep your hands where I can see them. I don't like surprises.
